page: Amazon Web Services m3.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core. It includes 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and operates on the Xen hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores. However, multi-core performance reaches average tiers in stress-ng and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth remains average, with cached read speeds of 386.65 GB/sec and uncached write speeds of 35.54 GB/sec. The instance demonstrates average throughput for static web serving at 6.7 GB/sec and software compilation, but falls into the lower tiers for database operations and Redis SET operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m3.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile of compute, memory, and local NVMe storage for legacy workloads, though its older hardware architecture limits its performance compared to newer generations. It is best suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, basic multi-threaded applications, and software compilation tasks that can utilize its 8 vCPUs and 30 GB of RAM without requiring high single-core clock speeds or modern instruction sets like AVX-512.
description: Amazon Web Services m3.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores, 30.0 GB of RAM, and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating under the Xen hypervisor, it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, though single-core performance and database operations are relatively weak. The instance achieves average throughput in static web serving and software compilation. Given its balanced resource allocation and older hardware profile, the m3.2xlarge is qualitatively cost-effective for legacy general-purpose workloads, basic multi-threaded applications, and static web serving that do not demand high single-thread performance or modern CPU instruction sets.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m3.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and 160 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for legacy workloads and static web serving.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated legacy Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe SSD storage, and stable multi-core performance.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon E5-2670 v2 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
30.0 GB of system memory and 160 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Xen hypervisor
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and legacy applications
